title: Selinum sisarum
taxonomy: Selinum sisarum is classified within the plant kingdom, belonging to the family Apiaceae, which is commonly known as the carrot or parsley family. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, such as its leaf and stem morphology, and its reproductive structures. Within the family Apiaceae, Selinum sisarum is placed in the genus Selinum, which includes several species of fern-like plants. The specific classification of Selinum sisarum is further specified as Selinum sisarum (L.) Koso-Pol. This classification reflects the plant's evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other members of the family.

intro: Selinum carvifolia, also known as Selinum sisarum, is a perennial plant belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae). The plant is valued for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, making it a potential treatment for various ailments such as digestive issues and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Selinum sisarum include sesquiterpenes, coumarins, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Selinum sisarum is commonly used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat digestive problems, skin conditions, and as a natural remedy for colds and fever.
